title: Intensely Rich Dutch Chicken Stew
description: A rich and juicy Dutch dish perfect for a lazy Sunday, featuring unwaxed lemon and flambe techniques, and high in protein for a satisfying meal.
instructions: Melt 2 tablespoons of butter in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the chicken thighs to the pot, browning them on all sides, then remove them from the pot and set aside.
Add the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter to the pot, then sprinkle the flour over the butter, whisking to combine.
Cook the flour mixture for 1-2 minutes, or until it is lightly golden and has a nutty aroma.
Add the chicken broth, heavy cream, dried thyme, salt, and black pepper to the pot, whisking to combine.
Return the chicken thighs to the pot, making sure they are covered with the sauce.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 30 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through.
Stir in the lemon juice and flambe the mixture by igniting the sauce with a match, allowing the flames to cook for 30 seconds to 1 minute.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
